quiz.title: Geometry: End-of-Year Review — Similarity and Trigonometry
quiz.subtitle: Unit 12 Quiz 8: Comprehensive Review of Units 7 and 8 (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 12.8 End-of-Year Review: Similarity and Trigonometry

questions.question: In $\triangle ABC$, $\overline{DE} \parallel \overline{BC}$, with $D$ on $\overline{AB}$ and $E$ on $\overline{AC}$. If $AD = 6$, $DB = 9$, and $AE = 8$, what is $EC$?
questions.explanation: By the Triangle Proportionality Theorem: $\frac{AD}{DB} = \frac{AE}{EC}$. Substituting: $\frac{6}{9} = \frac{8}{EC}$, so $\frac{2}{3} = \frac{8}{EC}$. Cross-multiplying: $2 \cdot EC = 24$, giving $EC = 12$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle ABC$ with the right angle at $C$, altitude $\overline{CD}$ is drawn to hypotenuse $\overline{AB}$. If $AD = 4$ and $DB = 16$, what is $CD$?
questions.explanation: The altitude to the hypotenuse is the geometric mean of the two segments of the hypotenuse: $CD = \sqrt{AD \cdot DB} = \sqrt{4 \cdot 16} = \sqrt{64} = 8$.

questions.question: In $\triangle PQR$, $\overline{PS}$ bisects $\angle P$, with $S$ on $\overline{QR}$. If $PQ = 15$, $PR = 20$, and $QS = 9$, what is $SR$?
questions.explanation: By the Angle Bisector Theorem: $\frac{QS}{SR} = \frac{PQ}{PR}$. Substituting: $\frac{9}{SR} = \frac{15}{20} = \frac{3}{4}$. Cross-multiplying: $3 \cdot SR = 36$, so $SR = 12$.

questions.question: In a $30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle, the shortest side is $5$. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: In a $30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle, the ratio is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$ (shortest : middle : hypotenuse). The shortest side (opposite $30^\circ$) is $5$, so the hypotenuse is $2 \times 5 = 10$. The middle side would be $5\sqrt{3}$.

questions.question: In a $45^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle, the hypotenuse is $8\sqrt{2}$. What is the length of each leg?
questions.explanation: In a $45^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$90^\circ$ triangle, the ratio is $1 : 1 : \sqrt{2}$ (leg : leg : hypotenuse). If the hypotenuse is $x\sqrt{2} = 8\sqrt{2}$, then $x = 8$. Each leg is $8$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle ABC$ with the right angle at $C$, $\sin A = \frac{5}{13}$. If $BC = 10$, what is the length of the hypotenuse $AB$?
A $20$-foot ladder leans against a wall, making a $60^\circ$ angle with the ground. How high up the wall does the ladder reach, in exact form?
questions.explanation: The height is opposite the $60^\circ$ angle. $\sin(60^\circ) = \frac{\text{height}}{20}$, so height $= 20 \cdot \sin(60^\circ) = 20 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = 10\sqrt{3}$ feet.

questions.question: In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle A = 40^\circ$, $\angle B = 70^\circ$, and side $c$ (opposite $\angle C$) $= 30$. Using the Law of Sines, find side $a$ (opposite $\angle A$), rounded to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: $\angle C = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 70^\circ = 70^\circ$. Law of Sines: $\frac{\sin A}{a} = \frac{\sin C}{c}$, so $\frac{\sin 40^\circ}{a} = \frac{\sin 70^\circ}{30}$. Then $a = \frac{30 \cdot \sin 40^\circ}{\sin 70^\circ} \approx \frac{30 \cdot 0.6428}{0.9397} \approx 20.5$.

questions.question: In $\triangle XYZ$, $x = 10$, $y = 14$, and $\angle Z = 55^\circ$. Using the Law of Cosines, find side $z$ (opposite $\angle Z$), rounded to the nearest tenth.
Two similar triangles have corresponding sides in the ratio $3:7$. If the area of the smaller triangle is $45$ cm$^2$, what is the area of the larger triangle?
questions.explanation: Area ratio $= k^2 = (\frac{7}{3})^2 = \frac{49}{9}$. Larger area $= 45 \times \frac{49}{9} = 5 \times 49 = 245$ cm$^2$. The larger triangle's sides are $\frac{7}{3}$ times bigger, but its area is $\frac{49}{9}$ times bigger.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the core concepts of Units 7 and 8: triangle proportionality, geometric mean in right triangles, special right triangles, trigonometric ratios, Law of Sines, Law of Cosines, and similarity with scale factors.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review: (1) Triangle Proportionality: parallel lines divide sides proportionally, (2) Altitude to hypotenuse is geometric mean of the segments, (3) $30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$: $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$, $45^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$90^\circ$: $1 : 1 : \sqrt{2}$, (4) Law of Sines for AAS/ASA, Law of Cosines for SAS/SSS.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: (1) setting up proportions correctly — match corresponding parts, (2) geometric mean: $\sqrt{a \cdot b}$, (3) SOH CAH TOA and special right triangle ratios, (4) area scales by $k^2$ when sides scale by $k$, (5) Law of Sines: $\frac{\sin A}{a} = \frac{\sin B}{b} = \frac{\sin C}{c}$.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Unit 7: Triangle Proportionality Theorem, Angle Bisector Theorem, geometric mean in right triangles, similar figures ($k$ for lengths, $k^2$ for areas). Unit 8: Pythagorean Theorem, special right triangles ($30^\circ$-$60^\circ$-$90^\circ$ and $45^\circ$-$45^\circ$-$90^\circ$), SOH CAH TOA, Law of Sines, Law of Cosines, and real-world applications.
